description_long: This is a short wooden fence constructed from thick, natural-looking timber beams. The fence consists of two sturdy vertical posts on either end, each reinforced with diagonal support braces for stability. Two horizontal rails, each with evenly spaced wooden pegs or dowels, connect the posts and add a rustic, handcrafted appearance. The wood grain is clearly visible, and the posts are capped with flat tops, while the base of each post features a metallic band for added durability. The overall design is simple and functional, suitable for use as a barrier in parks, gardens, or along sidewalks. There are no decorative elements, and the structure is robust but not overly tall, making it easy to see over.
description: A short, sturdy wooden fence with two thick posts, two horizontal rails, and visible wood grain, reinforced by diagonal braces and metal bands at the base.
description_view_0: Front view showing both vertical posts, two horizontal rails with pegs, and the full width of the fence.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the posts, the diagonal support brace, and the metal band at the base.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the posts, rails, and pegs, but with less detail on the wood grain.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the post thickness, diagonal brace, and metal band, mirroring view 1.
